evals.prompt: Plan the multi-slot booking system for a room-sharing platform. Users need to book 2+ time slots atomically with rollback on partial failure. We use Next.js, Supabase, and PostGIS.
evals.expected_output: A confidence-scored plan with numbered implementation steps, file paths, verification criteria, and zero unverified assumptions.
expectations: Asks product review questions before technical planning (what problem, who, why now)
Scores request clarity across dimensions (scope, success criteria, constraints, context, non-goals)
Classifies task type as IMPLEMENT
Performs web research on booking patterns (Stripe, Airbnb, state machines)
Assembles 5-6 task-adapted agents including Harsh Critic and Assumption Auditor
Produces numbered implementation steps with specific file paths
Each step has a verification criterion (how to check it worked)
Includes a confidence score with 6 weighted dimensions
Contains an assumption audit table with zero unverified assumptions
Harsh Critic verdict is PASS or CONDITIONAL PASS with severity ratings
Pre-mortem analysis covers at least 3 failure categories
Saves artifacts to .deliberate-plan/artifacts/

evals.prompt: Fix the race condition where two users can book the same slot simultaneously. The booking table is in Supabase with RLS enabled.
evals.expected_output: A FIX-type plan that diagnoses the race condition, researches Supabase locking patterns, and produces a specific fix plan.
expectations: Classifies task type as FIX
Asks targeted diagnostic questions (reproduction steps, frequency, affected users)
Researches Supabase row-level locking and SELECT FOR UPDATE patterns
Reads the actual codebase to find the booking endpoint
Assembles FIX-appropriate agents (Debugger, Backend Developer, etc.)
Plan includes specific file paths for the fix
Addresses the root cause (not just symptoms)
Includes rollback strategy if fix causes new issues
Confidence score reflects certainty about root cause

evals.prompt: Design the architecture for adding real-time notifications when a booking is confirmed, cancelled, or modified. Should work across web and mobile.
evals.expected_output: A DESIGN-type plan covering WebSocket vs SSE vs push, with architecture diagram description and implementation steps.
expectations: Classifies task type as DESIGN
Asks domain preferences (WebSocket vs SSE vs polling, push notification service)
Researches real-time notification patterns for Next.js + Supabase
Assembles DESIGN-appropriate agents (Architect, Frontend, Backend, Mobile)
Debates trade-offs between approaches (at least 2 alternatives compared)
Plan covers both web and mobile as specified
Includes scaling considerations
Addresses offline/reconnection scenarios
